NKJV.1: God, who at various times and in various ways spoke in time past to the fathers by the prophets,
NKJV.2: has in these last days spoken to us by His Son, whom He has appointed heir of all things, through whom also He made the worlds;
NKJV.3: who being the brightness of His glory and the express image of His person, and upholding all things by the word of His power, when He had by Himself purged our sins, sat down at the right hand of the Majesty on high,
NKJV.4: having become so much better than the angels, as He has by inheritance obtained a more excellent name than they.
NKJV.5: For to which of the angels did He ever say:  “You are My Son, Today I have begotten You”?  And again:  “I will be to Him a Father, And He shall be to Me a Son”?
NKJV.6: But when He again brings the firstborn into the world, He says:  “Let all the angels of God worship Him.”
NKJV.7: And of the angels He says:  “Who makes His angels spirits And His ministers a flame of fire.”
NKJV.8: But to the Son He says: “Your throne, O God, is forever and ever; A scepter of righteousness is the scepter of Your kingdom.
NKJV.9: You have loved righteousness and hated lawlessness; Therefore God, Your God, has anointed You With the oil of gladness more than Your companions.”
NKJV.10: And: “You, LORD, in the beginning laid the foundation of the earth, And the heavens are the work of Your hands.
NKJV.11: They will perish, but You remain; And they will all grow old like a garment;
NKJV.12: Like a cloak You will fold them up, And they will be changed. But You are the same, And Your years will not fail.”
NKJV.13: But to which of the angels has He ever said:  “Sit at My right hand, Till I make Your enemies Your footstool”?
NKJV.14: Are they not all ministering spirits sent forth to minister for those who will inherit salvation?

ESV.1: Long ago, at many times and in many ways, God spoke to our fathers by the prophets,
ESV.2: but in these last days he has spoken to us by his Son, whom he appointed the heir of all things, through whom also he created the world.
ESV.3: He is the radiance of the glory of God and the exact imprint of his nature, and he upholds the universe by the word of his power. After making purification for sins, he sat down at the right hand of the Majesty on high,
ESV.4: having become as much superior to angels as the name he has inherited is more excellent than theirs.
ESV.5: For to which of the angels did God ever say, “You are my Son, today I have begotten you”?  Or again, “I will be to him a father, and he shall be to me a son”?
ESV.6: And again, when he brings the firstborn into the world, he says, “Let all God's angels worship him.”
ESV.7: Of the angels he says, “He makes his angels winds, and his ministers a flame of fire.”
ESV.8: But of the Son he says, “Your throne, O God, is forever and ever, the scepter of uprightness is the scepter of your kingdom.
ESV.9: You have loved righteousness and hated wickedness; therefore God, your God, has anointed you with the oil of gladness beyond your companions.”
ESV.10: And, “You, Lord, laid the foundation of the earth in the beginning, and the heavens are the work of your hands;
ESV.11: they will perish, but you remain; they will all wear out like a garment,
ESV.12: like a robe you will roll them up, like a garment they will be changed.  But you are the same, and your years will have no end.”
ESV.13: And to which of the angels has he ever said, “Sit at my right hand until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet”?
ESV.14: Are they not all ministering spirits sent out to serve for the sake of those who are to inherit salvation?

NASB.1: God, after He spoke long ago to the fathers in the prophets in many portions and in many ways,
NASB.2: in these last days has spoken to us in His Son, whom He appointed heir of all things, through whom also He made the world.
NASB.3: And He is the radiance of His glory and the exact representation of His nature, and upholds all things by the word of His power. When He had made purification of sins, He sat down at the right hand of the Majesty on high,
NASB.4: having become as much better than the angels, as He has inherited a more excellent name than they.
NASB.5: For to which of the angels did He ever say, 'YOU ARE MY SON, TODAY I HAVE BEGOTTEN YOU'? And again, 'I WILL BE A FATHER TO HIM AND HE SHALL BE A SON TO ME'?
NASB.6: And when He again brings the firstborn into the world, He says, 'AND LET ALL THE ANGELS OF GOD WORSHIP HIM.'
NASB.7: And of the angels He says, 'WHO MAKES HIS ANGELS WINDS, AND HIS MINISTERS A FLAME OF FIRE.'
NASB.8: But of the Son [He says], 'YOUR THRONE, O GOD, IS FOREVER AND EVER, AND THE RIGHTEOUS SCEPTER IS THE SCEPTER OF HIS KINGDOM.
NASB.9: 'YOU HAVE LOVED RIGHTEOUSNESS AND HATED LAWLESSNESS; THEREFORE GOD, YOUR GOD, HAS ANOINTED YOU WITH THE OIL OF GLADNESS ABOVE YOUR COMPANIONS.'
NASB.10: And, 'YOU, LORD, IN THE BEGINNING LAID THE FOUNDATION OF THE EARTH, AND THE HEAVENS ARE THE WORKS OF YOUR HANDS;
NASB.11: THEY WILL PERISH, BUT YOU REMAIN; AND THEY ALL WILL BECOME OLD LIKE A GARMENT,
NASB.12: AND LIKE A MANTLE YOU WILL ROLL THEM UP; LIKE A GARMENT THEY WILL ALSO BE CHANGED. BUT YOU ARE THE SAME, AND YOUR YEARS WILL NOT COME TO AN END.'
NASB.13: But to which of the angels has He ever said, 'SIT AT MY RIGHT HAND, UNTIL I MAKE YOUR ENEMIES A FOOTSTOOL FOR YOUR FEET '?
NASB.14: Are they not all ministering spirits, sent out to render service for the sake of those who will inherit salvation?

NLT.1: Long ago God spoke many times and in many ways to our ancestors through the prophets.
NLT.2: And now in these final days, he has spoken to us through his Son. God promised everything to the Son as an inheritance, and through the Son he created the universe.
NLT.3: The Son radiates God’s own glory and expresses the very character of God, and he sustains everything by the mighty power of his command. When he had cleansed us from our sins, he sat down in the place of honor at the right hand of the majestic God in heaven.
NLT.4: This shows that the Son is far greater than the angels, just as the name God gave him is greater than their names.
NLT.5:
For God never said to any angel what he said to Jesus: “You are my Son.
Today I have become your Father. ”
God also said, “I will be his Father,
and he will be my Son.”
NLT.6: And when he brought his supreme Son into the world, God said, “Let all of God’s angels worship him.”
NLT.7:
Regarding the angels, he says, “He sends his angels like the winds,
his servants like flames of fire.”
NLT.8:
But to the Son he says, “Your throne, O God, endures forever and ever.
You rule with a scepter of justice.
NLT.9:
You love justice and hate evil.
Therefore, O God, your God has anointed you,
pouring out the oil of joy on you more than on anyone else.”
NLT.10:
He also says to the Son, “In the beginning, Lord, you laid the foundation of the earth
and made the heavens with your hands.
NLT.11:
They will perish, but you remain forever.
They will wear out like old clothing.
NLT.12:
You will fold them up like a cloak
and discard them like old clothing.
But you are always the same;
you will live forever.”
NLT.13:
And God never said to any of the angels, “Sit in the place of honor at my right hand
until I humble your enemies,
making them a footstool under your feet.”
NLT.14: Therefore, angels are only servants — spirits sent to care for people who will inherit salvation.

WEB.1: God, having in the past spoken to the fathers through the prophets at many times and in various ways,
WEB.2: has at the end of these days spoken to us by his Son, whom he appointed heir of all things, through whom also he made the worlds.
WEB.3: His Son is the radiance of his glory, the very image of his substance, and upholding all things by the word of his power, when he had by himself purified us of our sins, sat down on the right hand of the Majesty on high;
WEB.4: having become so much better than the angels, as he has inherited a more excellent name than they have.
WEB.5: For to which of the angels did he say at any time, “You are my Son. Today I have become your father?”   and again, “I will be to him a Father, and he will be to me a Son?”
WEB.6: When he again brings in the firstborn into the world he says, “Let all the angels of God worship him.”
WEB.7: Of the angels he says, “Who makes his angels winds, and his servants a flame of fire.”
WEB.8: But of the Son he says, “Your throne, O God, is forever and ever. The scepter of uprightness is the scepter of your Kingdom.
WEB.9: You have loved righteousness, and hated iniquity; therefore God, your God, has anointed you with the oil of gladness above your fellows.”
WEB.10: And, “You, Lord, in the beginning, laid the foundation of the earth. The heavens are the works of your hands.
WEB.11: They will perish, but you continue. They all will grow old like a garment does.
WEB.12: You will roll them up like a mantle, and they will be changed; but you are the same. Your years will not fail.”
WEB.13: But which of the angels has he told at any time, “Sit at my right hand, until I make your enemies the footstool of your feet?”
WEB.14: Aren’t they all serving spirits, sent out to do service for the sake of those who will inherit salvation?

CSB17.1: Long ago God spoke <sup>ⓘ</sup> to the fathers by the prophets <sup>ⓙ</sup> at different times and in different ways. <sup>ⓚ</sup>
CSB17.2: In these last days, <sup>ⓛ</sup> he has spoken to us by his Son. God has appointed him heir of all things <sup>ⓜ</sup> and made the universe <sup>[1]</sup> <sup>ⓝ</sup> through him.
CSB17.3: The Son is the radiance <sup>[2]</sup> of God’s glory and the exact expression <sup>[3]</sup> of his nature, <sup>ⓐ</sup> sustaining all things by his powerful word. <sup>ⓑ</sup> After making purification for sins, <sup>[4]</sup> he sat down at the right hand of the Majesty on high. <sup>[5]</sup> <sup>ⓒ</sup>
CSB17.4: So he became superior to the angels, just as the name he inherited is more excellent than theirs. <sup>ⓓ</sup>

and his servants [11] a fiery flame, ⓘ ⓙ

is forever and ever,

AMP.1: God, having spoken to the fathers long ago in [the voices and writings of] the prophets in many separate revelations [each of which set forth a portion of the truth], and in many ways,
AMP.2: has in these last days spoken [with finality] to us in [the person of One who is by His character and nature] His Son [namely Jesus], whom He appointed heir and lawful owner of all things, through whom also He created the universe [that is, the universe as a space-time-matter continuum].
AMP.3: The Son is the radiance and only expression of the glory of [our awesome] God [reflecting God's Shekinah glory, the Light-being, the brilliant light of the divine], and the exact representation and perfect imprint of His [Father's] essence, and upholding and maintaining and propelling all things [the entire physical and spiritual universe] by His powerful word [carrying the universe along to its predetermined goal]. When He [Himself and no other] had [by offering Himself on the cross as a sacrifice for sin] accomplished purification from sins and established our freedom from guilt, He sat down [revealing His completed work] at the right hand of the Majesty on high [revealing His Divine authority],
AMP.4: having become as much superior to angels, since He has inherited a more excellent and glorious name than they [that is, Son—the name above all names].
AMP.5:
For to which of the angels did the Father ever say,
"You are My Son,
Today I have begotten (fathered) You [established You as a Son, with kingly dignity]"?
And again [did He ever say to the angels],
"I shall be a Father to Him
And He shall be a Son to Me"?
AMP.6:
And when He again brings the firstborn [highest-ranking Son] into the world, He says,
"And all the angels of God are to worship Him."
AMP.7:
And concerning the angels He says,
"Who makes His angels winds,
And His ministering servants flames of fire [to do His bidding]."
AMP.8:
But about the Son [the Father says to Him],
"Your throne, O God, is forever and ever,
And thescepter of [absolute] righteousness is the scepter ofHis kingdom.
AMP.9:
"You have loved righteousness [integrity, virtue, uprightness in purpose] and have hated lawlessness [injustice, sin].
Therefore God, Your God, Has anointed You
With the oil of gladness above Your companions."
AMP.10:
And,
"You, Lord, laid the foundation of the earth in the beginning,
And the heavens are the works of Your hands;
AMP.11:
They will perish, but You remain [forever and ever];
And they will all wear out like a garment,
AMP.12:
And like a robe You will roll them up;
Like a garment they will be changed.
ButYou are the same [forever],
And Your years will never end."
AMP.13:
But to which of the angels has the Father ever said,
"Sit at My right hand [together with me in royal dignity],
Until I make your enemies
A footstool for your feet [in triumphant conquest]"?
AMP.14: Are not all the angels ministering spirits sent out [by God] to serve (accompany, protect) those who will inherit salvation? [Of course they are!]

MSG.1: Going through a long line of prophets, God has been addressing our ancestors in different ways for centuries.
MSG.2: Recently he spoke to us directly through his Son. By his Son, God created the world in the beginning, and it will all belong to the Son at the end.
MSG.3: This Son perfectly mirrors God, and is stamped with God's nature. He holds everything together by what he says—powerful words! After he finished the sacrifice for sins, the Son took his honored place high in the heavens right alongside God,
MSG.4: far higher than any angel in rank and rule.
MSG.5: Did God ever say to an angel, "You're my Son; today I celebrate you"? Or, "I'm his Father, he's my Son"?
MSG.6: When he presents his honored Son to the world, he says, "All angels must worship him."
MSG.7: Regarding angels he says, The messengers are winds, the servants are tongues of fire.
MSG.8: But he says to the Son, You're God, and on the throne for good; your rule makes everything right.
MSG.9: You love it when things are right; you hate it when things are wrong. That is why God, your God, poured fragrant oil on your head, Marking you out as king, far above your dear companions.
MSG.10: And again to the Son, You, Master, started it all, laid earth's foundations, then crafted the stars in the sky.
MSG.11: Earth and sky will wear out, but not you; they become threadbare like an old coat;
MSG.12: You'll fold them up like a worn-out cloak, and lay them away on the shelf. But you'll stay the same, year after year; you'll never fade, you'll never wear out.
MSG.13: And did he ever say anything like this to an angel? Sit alongside me here on my throne Until I make your enemies a stool for your feet.
MSG.14: Isn't it obvious that all angels are sent to help out with those lined up to receive salvation?

NIV.1:
The Son Superior to Angels
In the past God spoke to our forefathers through the prophets at many times and in various ways,
NIV.2: but in these last days he has spoken to us by his Son, whom he appointed heir of all things, and through whom he made the universe.
NIV.3: The Son is the radiance of God’s glory and the exact representation of his being, sustaining all things by his powerful word. After he had provided purification for sins, he sat down at the right hand of the Majesty in heaven.
NIV.4: So he became as much superior to the angels as the name he has inherited is superior to theirs.
NIV.5:
For to which of the angels did God ever say,
“You are my Son;
today I have become your Father”?
Or again,
“I will be his Father,
and he will be my Son”?
NIV.6:
And again, when God brings his firstborn into the world, he says,
“Let all God’s angels worship him.”
NIV.7:
In speaking of the angels he says,
“He makes his angels winds,
his servants flames of fire.”
NIV.8:
But about the Son he says,
“Your throne, O God, will last for ever and ever,
and righteousness will be the scepter of your kingdom.
NIV.9:
You have loved righteousness and hated wickedness;
therefore God, your God, has set you above your companions
by anointing you with the oil of joy.”
NIV.10:
He also says,
“In the beginning, O Lord, you laid the foundations of the earth,
and the heavens are the work of your hands.
NIV.11:
They will perish, but you remain;
they will all wear out like a garment.
NIV.12:
You will roll them up like a robe;
like a garment they will be changed.
But you remain the same,
and your years will never end.”
NIV.13:
To which of the angels did God ever say,
“Sit at my right hand
until I make your enemies
a footstool for your feet”?
NIV.14: Are not all angels ministering spirits sent to serve those who will inherit salvation?

NRSVCE.1: Long ago God spoke to our ancestors in many and various ways by the prophets,
NRSVCE.2: but in these last days he has spoken to us by a Son, whom he appointed heir of all things, through whom he also created the worlds.
NRSVCE.3: He is the reflection of God’s glory and the exact imprint of God’s very being, and he sustains all things by his powerful word. When he had made purification for sins, he sat down at the right hand of the Majesty on high,
NRSVCE.4: having become as much superior to angels as the name he has inherited is more excellent than theirs.
NRSVCE.5:
For to which of the angels did God ever say, “You are my Son;
today I have begotten you”?
Or again, “I will be his Father,
and he will be my Son”?
NRSVCE.6: And again, when he brings the firstborn into the world, he says, “Let all God’s angels worship him.”
NRSVCE.7:
Of the angels he says, “He makes his angels winds,
and his servants flames of fire.”
NRSVCE.8:
But of the Son he says, “Your throne, O God, is forever and ever,
and the righteous scepter is the scepter of your kingdom.
NRSVCE.9:
You have loved righteousness and hated wickedness;
therefore God, your God, has anointed you
with the oil of gladness beyond your companions.”
NRSVCE.10:
And, “In the beginning, Lord, you founded the earth,
and the heavens are the work of your hands;
NRSVCE.11:
they will perish, but you remain;
they will all wear out like clothing;
NRSVCE.12:
like a cloak you will roll them up,
and like clothing they will be changed.
But you are the same,
and your years will never end.”
NRSVCE.13:
But to which of the angels has he ever said, “Sit at my right hand
until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet”?
NRSVCE.14: Are not all angels spirits in the divine service, sent to serve for the sake of those who are to inherit salvation?

NET.1: After God spoke long ago in various portions and in various ways to our ancestors through the prophets,
NET.2: in these last days he has spoken to us in a son, whom he appointed heir of all things, and through whom he created the world.
NET.3: The Son is the radiance of his glory and the representation of his essence, and he sustains all things by his powerful word, and so when he had accomplished cleansing for sins, he sat down at the right hand of the Majesty on high.
NET.4: Thus he became so far better than the angels as he has inherited a name superior to theirs.
NET.5: For to which of the angels did God ever say, “You are my son! Today I have fathered you”? And in another place he says, “I will be his father and he will be my son.”
NET.6: But when he again brings his firstborn into the world, he says, “Let all the angels of God worship him!”
NET.7: And he says of the angels, “He makes his angels spirits and his ministers a flame of fire,”
NET.8: but of the Son he says, “Your throne, O God, is forever and ever, and a righteous scepter is the scepter of your kingdom.
NET.9: You have loved righteousness and hated lawlessness. So God, your God, has anointed you over your companions with the oil of rejoicing.”
NET.10: And, “You founded the earth in the beginning, Lord, and the heavens are the works of your hands.
NET.11: They will perish, but you continue. And they will all grow old like a garment,
NET.12: and like a robe you will fold them up and like a garment they will be changed, but you are the same and your years will never run out.”
NET.13: But to which of the angels has he ever said, “Sit at my right hand until I make your enemies a footstool for your feet”?
NET.14: Are they not all ministering spirits, sent out to serve those who will inherit salvation?
